instructions: Use package comments as the source of truth for package ownership boundaries. Flag changes that move GCE VM lifecycle, GKE cluster API reads, source node-pool selection, or provider policy into the wrong package. For pkg/metadata specifically, enforce that it remains a pure typed bootstrap metadata model and render boundary with no provider policy or API calls.

rules.rule: Provider changes should respect package ownership: instance owns GCE VM lifecycle, instance labels/tags, scheduling, capacity errors, zone operations, and provider-owned bootstrap policy; nodepooltemplate owns bootstrap source pool/template discovery; gke owns shared GKE cluster facts; pkg/metadata owns pure GKE bootstrap metadata modeling, parsing, typed mutation helpers, and Compute API rendering.

pkg/metadata must remain a pure transformation layer: no GCP API calls, no Kubernetes API calls, no source node-pool/template discovery, no GCE VM lifecycle decisions, no scheduler/capacity policy, and no in-place mutation of caller-owned Compute metadata templates.

Provider-owned bootstrap policy belongs outside pkg/metadata. Labels, taints, provisioning model, GPU behavior, disk compatibility, secondary boot disks, kubelet defaults, Spot shutdown behavior, NodeClass overlays, and source-template selection should be decided by provider packages and passed into pkg/metadata through typed helpers.
